PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 395.5, VENUE IS PROPER IN THE COUNTY WHERE THE CORPORATION'S PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IS LOCATED, WHERE THE CONTRACT IS MADE OR IS TO BE PERFORMED, OR WHERE THE BREACH OCCURS. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action
Off Calendar Mar-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RONALD K. LOSCH OF THE LOSCH & EHRLICH LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 956-8400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN G. GABRIEL OF THE GABRIEL SALOMONS LLP, TEL. NO. (818) 906-3700 EXT. 302 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NATIONAL MERCHANT CENTER , INCORPORATED'S Motion To Transfer Action is Granted. This case is transferred to Orange County. Pursuant to CCP sec. 395.5, venue is proper in the county where the corporation's principal place of business is located, where the contract is made or is to be performed, or where the breach occurs. It is undisputed that Defendant's principal place of business is Orange County. Paragraph 4 of the Declaration of Roman Balanko establishes that the contract was entered into in Orange County because that is where "the defendant's obligation was incurred." (Braunstein v. Sup. Ct. (1964) 225 Cal.App.2d 691, 694.) The contract provides that the services were to be provided in Orange County. (Complaint, Ex. B, Telekenex Information Services Supplement, par. 5.) The alleged breach occurred in Los Angeles County, where Plaintiff admits payment was due. (Declaration of Marilou King, par. 8.) Thus, there is no basis for venue in San Francisco County. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/ehg
